dialogueChunks.intro: The deadline moved up, {player_name}. More people fell overnight. The council says they can't wait any longer.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} found a partial solution in the records but it requires {quest_item} from {location_hint}. It won't save everyone: maybe half, maybe fewer. The council is debating whether a partial solution justifies keeping the doors open. The sickest among them told me to let the council seal it.
dialogueChunks.outro: I can't accept that. Get {quest_item} to {target_npc}. Even saving half is better than losing all of them.

playerResponses.text: Half is better than none. I'll get {quest_item}.
They might be right. Sometimes love means letting go.

dialogueChunks.intro: The rescue plan hit a wall. The {enemy_type} moved the captives deeper into their territory. {quest_ally} barely made it back alive.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} intercepted a message: the {enemy_type} are splitting the hostages into two groups at different locations. We can reach one in time but not both. {quest_ally} says {quest_stakes} is at risk no matter what we choose.
dialogueChunks.outro: Choose which group to save and someone dies. Refuse to choose and everyone dies. Find {quest_item}: it might give us the edge to reach both, but it means going through hostile territory first.

playerResponses.text: I'll find a way to reach both. No one gets left behind.
The numbers are clear. Save the larger group.

dialogueChunks.intro: {quest_focus} is weakening on its own, {player_name}. We might not even need to destroy it: it might collapse by itself.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} found an alternate route downstream, but {enemy_type} scouts are already patrolling it. The stranded people include children and elders who can barely walk. Getting them to safety means moving slowly through open ground.
dialogueChunks.outro: Clear the patrols and mark the path with {quest_item}. If {quest_focus} fails before everyone crosses, the alternate route is their only chance.

playerResponses.text: I'll clear the path. Get those people moving.
Can {quest_focus} hold long enough for them to cross normally?
A few people against an army. You know what the math says.
